heading: Assistance to combat HIV/AIDS

Amendments2013—Subsec. (f). amended subsec. (f) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (f) related to annual reports on the implementation of this section.Pub. L. 113–562008—Subsec. (a). , inserted “Central Asia, Eastern Europe, Latin America” after “Caribbean,”.Pub. L. 110–293, § 301(a)(1)Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “It is a major objective of the foreign assistance program of the United States to provide assistance for the prevention, treatment, and control of HIV/AIDS. The United States and other developed countries should provide assistance to countries in sub-Saharan Africa, the Caribbean, and other countries and areas to control this crisis through HIV/AIDS prevention, treatment, monitoring, and related activities, particularly activities focused on women and youth, including strategies to protect women and prevent mother-to-child transmission of the HIV infection.”Pub. Delegation of FunctionsFor delegation of functions of President under this section, see Ex. Ord. No. 12163, , 44 F.R. 56673, as amended, set out as a note under .Sept. 29, 1979section 2381 of this title

children.heading: Finding
children.label: a Finding
children.text: Congress recognizes that the alarming spread of HIV/AIDS in countries in sub-Saharan Africa, the Caribbean, Central Asia, Eastern Europe, Latin America and other developing countries is a major global health, national security, development, and humanitarian crisis.

children.label: i
children.text: prevent 12,000,000 new HIV infections worldwide;

children.label: I
children.text: the increase in the number of individuals with HIV/AIDS receiving antiretroviral treatment above the goal established under section 7672(a)(3) of this title and increased pursuant to paragraphs (1) through (3) of section 7673(d) of this title; and11See References in Text note below.1

children.label: II
children.text: additional treatment through coordinated multilateral efforts;

children.label: iii
children.text: support care for 12,000,000 individuals infected with or affected by HIV/AIDS, including 5,000,000 orphans and vulnerable children affected by HIV/AIDS, with an emphasis on promoting a comprehensive, coordinated system of services to be integrated throughout the continuum of care;

children.label: iv
children.text: provide at least 80 percent of the target population with access to counseling, testing, and treatment to prevent the transmission of HIV from mother-to-child;

children.label: v
children.text: provide care and treatment services to children with HIV in proportion to their percentage within the HIV-infected population of a given partner country; and

children.label: vi
children.text: train and support retention of health care professionals, paraprofessionals, and community health workers in HIV/AIDS prevention, treatment, and care, with the target of providing such training to at least 140,000 new health care professionals and paraprofessionals with an emphasis on training and in country deployment of critically needed doctors and nurses;

children.label: B
children.text: strengthen the capacity to deliver primary health care in developing countries, especially in sub-Saharan Africa;

children.label: C
children.text: support and help countries in their efforts to achieve staffing levels of at least 2.3 doctors, nurses, and midwives per 1,000 population, as called for by the World Health Organization; and

children.label: D
children.text: help partner countries to develop independent, sustainable HIV/AIDS programs.

children.heading: Coordinated global strategy
children.label: 2 Coordinated global strategy
children.text: The United States and other countries with the sufficient capacity should provide assistance to countries in sub-Saharan Africa, the Caribbean, Central Asia, Eastern Europe, and Latin America, and other countries and regions confronting HIV/AIDS epidemics in a coordinated global strategy to help address generalized and concentrated epidemics through HIV/AIDS prevention, treatment, care, monitoring and evaluation, and related activities.

children.label: A
children.text: the prevention of the transmission of HIV;

children.label: B
children.text: moving toward universal access to HIV/AIDS prevention counseling and services;

children.label: C
children.text: the inclusion of cost sharing assurances that meet the requirements under ; andsection 2151h of this title

children.label: D
children.text: the inclusion of transition strategies to ensure sustainability of such programs and activities, including health care systems, under other international donor support, or budget support by respective foreign governments.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Consistent with , the President is authorized to furnish assistance, on such terms and conditions as the President may determine, for HIV/AIDS, including to prevent, treat, and monitor HIV/AIDS, and carry out related activities, in countries in sub-Saharan Africa, the Caribbean, Central Asia, Eastern Europe, Latin America, and other countries and areas, particularly with respect to refugee populations or those in postconflict settings in such countries and areas with significant or increasing HIV incidence rates.section 2151b(c) of this title

children.heading: Role of NGOs
children.label: 2 Role of NGOs
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that the President should provide an appropriate level of assistance under paragraph (1) through nongovernmental organizations (including faith-based and community-based organizations) in countries in sub-Saharan Africa, the Caribbean, Central Asia, Eastern Europe, Latin America, and other countries and areas affected by the HIV/AIDS pandemic, particularly with respect to refugee populations or those in post-conflict settings in such countries and areas with significant or increasing HIV incidence rates..2So in original.2

children.heading: Coordination of assistance efforts
children.label: 3 Coordination of assistance efforts
children.text: The President shall coordinate the provision of assistance under paragraph (1) with the provision of related assistance by the Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS), the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF), the World Health Organization (WHO), the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria and other appropriate international organizations (such as the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development), relevant regional multilateral development institutions, national, state, and local governments of partner countries, other international actors,, appropriate governmental and nongovernmental organizations, and relevant executive branch agencies within the framework of the principles of the Three Ones.2

children.label: A
children.text: programs and efforts that are designed or intended to impart knowledge with the exclusive purpose of helping individuals avoid behaviors that place them at risk of HIV infection, including integration of such programs into health programs and the inclusion in counseling programs of information on methods of avoiding infection of HIV, including delaying sexual debut, abstinence, fidelity and monogamy, reduction of casual sexual partnering and multiple concurrent sexual partnering,, reducing sexual violence and coercion, including child marriage, widow inheritance, and polygamy, and where appropriate, use of male and female condoms;2

children.label: B
children.text: assistance to establish and implement culturally appropriate HIV/AIDS education and prevention programs that are designed with local input and focus on helping individuals avoid infection of HIV/AIDS, implemented through nongovernmental organizations, including faith-based and community-based organizations, particularly those locally based organizations that utilize both professionals and volunteers with appropriate skills, experience, and community presence;

children.label: C
children.text: assistance for the purpose of encouraging men to be responsible in their sexual behavior, child rearing, and to respect women;

children.label: D
children.text: assistance for the purpose of providing voluntary testing and counseling (including the incorporation of confidentiality protections with respect to such testing and counseling) and promoting the use of provider-initiated or “opt-out” voluntary testing in accordance with World Health Organization guidelines;

children.label: E
children.text: assistance for the purpose of preventing mother-to-child transmission of the HIV infection, including medications to prevent such transmission and access to infant formula and other alternatives for infant feeding;

children.label: i
children.text: achieve the goal of reaching 80 percent of pregnant women for prevention and treatment of mother-to-child transmission of HIV in countries in which the United States is implementing HIV/AIDS programs by 2013; and

children.label: ii
children.text: promote infant feeding options and treatment protocols that meet the most recent criteria established by the World Health Organization;

children.label: G
children.text: medical male circumcision programs as part of national strategies to combat the transmission of HIV/AIDS;

children.label: H
children.text: assistance to ensure a safe blood supply and sterile medical equipment;

children.label: I
children.text: assistance to help avoid substance abuse and intravenous drug use that can lead to HIV infection;

children.label: J
children.text: assistance for the purpose of increasing women’s access to employment opportunities, income, productive resources, and microfinance programs, where appropriate.3So in original. The period probably should be “; and”.3

children.label: i
children.text: counseling and other services for the prevention of reinfection of individuals with HIV/AIDS;

children.label: I
children.text: life skills development for practicing abstinence and faithfulness;

children.label: II
children.text: reducing the number of sexual partners;

children.label: III
children.text: delaying sexual debut; and

children.label: IV
children.text: ensuring correct and consistent use of condoms;

children.label: iii
children.text: assistance to engage underlying vulnerabilities to HIV/AIDS, especially those of women and girls;

children.label: iv
children.text: assistance for appropriate HIV/AIDS education programs and training targeted to prevent the transmission of HIV among men who have sex with men;

children.label: v
children.text: assistance to provide male and female condoms;

children.label: vi
children.text: diagnosis and treatment of other sexually transmitted infections;

children.label: vii
children.text: strategies to address the stigma and discrimination that impede HIV/AIDS prevention efforts; and

children.label: viii
children.text: assistance to facilitate widespread access to microbicides for HIV prevention, if safe and effective products become available, including financial and technical support for culturally appropriate introductory programs, procurement, distribution, logistics management, program delivery, acceptability studies, provider training, demand generation, and postintroduction monitoring.

children.label: A
children.text: assistance to establish and implement programs to strengthen and broaden indigenous health care delivery systems and the capacity of such systems to deliver HIV/AIDS pharmaceuticals and otherwise provide for the treatment of individuals with HIV/AIDS, including clinical training for indigenous organizations and health care providers;

children.label: B
children.text: assistance to strengthen and expand hospice and palliative care programs to assist patients debilitated by HIV/AIDS, their families, and the primary caregivers of such patients, including programs that utilize faith-based and community-based organizations;

children.label: C
children.text: assistance for the purpose of the care and treatment of individuals with HIV/AIDS through the provision of pharmaceuticals, including antiretrovirals and other pharmaceuticals and therapies for the treatment of opportunistic infections, pain management, nutritional support, and other treatment modalities;

children.label: D
children.text: as part of care and treatment of HIV/AIDS, assistance (including prophylaxis and treatment) for common HIV/AIDS-related opportunistic infections for free or at a rate at which it is easily affordable to the individuals and populations being served;4So in original. The word “and” probably should appear.4

children.label: E
children.text: as part of care and treatment of HIV/AIDS, assistance or referral to available and adequately resourced service providers for nutritional support, including counseling and where necessary the provision of commodities, for persons meeting malnourishment criteria and their families;5So in original. The semicolon probably should be a period.5

children.label: A
children.text: With particular emphasis on specific populations that represent a particularly high risk of contracting or spreading HIV/AIDS, including those exploited through the sex trade, victims of rape and sexual assault, individuals already infected with HIV/AIDS, and in cases of occupational exposure of health care workers, assistance with efforts to reduce the risk of HIV/AIDS infection including post-exposure pharmaceutical prophylaxis, and necessary pharmaceuticals and commodities, including test kits, condoms, and, when proven effective, microbicides.

children.label: B
children.text: Bulk purchases of available test kits, condoms, and, when proven effective, microbicides that are intended to reduce the risk of HIV/AIDS transmission and for appropriate program support for the introduction and distribution of these commodities, as well as education and training on the use of the technologies.

children.label: A
children.text: monitoring to ensure that adequate controls are established and implemented to provide HIV/AIDS pharmaceuticals and other appropriate medicines to poor individuals with HIV/AIDS;

children.label: B
children.text: appropriate evaluation and surveillance activities;

children.label: C
children.text: monitoring to ensure that appropriate measures are being taken to maintain the sustainability of HIV/AIDS pharmaceuticals (especially antiretrovirals) and ensure that drug resistance is not compromising the benefits of such pharmaceuticals;

children.label: D
children.text: monitoring to ensure appropriate law enforcement officials are working to ensure that HIV/AIDS pharmaceuticals are not diminished through illegal counterfeiting or black market sales of such pharmaceuticals;

children.label: i
children.text: improve accountability, increase transparency, and ensure the delivery of evidence-based services through the collection, evaluation, and analysis of data regarding gender-responsive interventions, disaggregated by age and sex;

children.label: ii
children.text: identify and replicate effective models; and

children.label: iii
children.text: develop gender indicators to measure outcomes and the impacts of interventions; and

children.label: i
children.text: gather epidemiological and social science data on HIV; and

children.label: ii
children.text: evaluate the effectiveness of prevention efforts among men who have sex with men, with due consideration to stigma and risks associated with disclosure.

children.heading: Procurement
children.label: A Procurement
children.text: The procurement of HIV/AIDS pharmaceuticals, antiviral therapies, and other appropriate medicines, including medicines to treat opportunistic infections.

children.heading: Mechanisms for quality control and sustainable supply
children.label: B Mechanisms for quality control and sustainable supply
children.text: Mechanisms to ensure that such HIV/AIDS pharmaceuticals, antiretroviral therapies, and other appropriate medicines are quality-controlled and sustainably supplied.

children.heading: Mechanism to ensure cost-effective drug purchasing
children.label: C Mechanism to ensure cost-effective drug purchasing
i
children.text: the Food and Drug Administration;

children.label: ii
children.text: a stringent regulatory agency acceptable to the Secretary of Health and Human Services; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a quality assurance mechanism acceptable to the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Distribution
children.label: D Distribution
children.text: The distribution of such HIV/AIDS pharmaceuticals, antiviral therapies, and other appropriate medicines (including medicines to treat opportunistic infections) to qualified national, regional, or local organizations for the treatment of individuals with HIV/AIDS in accordance with appropriate HIV/AIDS testing and monitoring requirements and treatment protocols and for the prevention of mother-to-child transmission of the HIV infection.

children.label: A
children.text: the care and support of children who are orphaned by the HIV/AIDS pandemic, including services designed to care for orphaned children in a family environment which rely on extended family members;

children.label: B
children.text: improved infrastructure and institutional capacity to develop and manage education, prevention, and treatment programs, including training and the resources to collect and maintain accurate HIV surveillance data to target programs and measure the effectiveness of interventions;

children.label: C
children.text: vaccine research and development partnership programs with specific plans of action to develop a safe, effective, accessible, preventive HIV vaccine for use throughout the world; and6So in original. The word “and” probably should not appear.6

children.label: i
children.text: enhance the clinical impact of HIV/AIDS care and treatment; and

children.label: I
children.text: nutritional and food support;

children.label: II
children.text: safe drinking water and adequate sanitation;

children.label: III
children.text: nutritional counseling;

children.label: IV
children.text: income-generating activities and livelihood initiatives;

children.label: V
children.text: maternal and child health care;

children.label: VI
children.text: primary health care;

children.label: VII
children.text: the diagnosis and treatment of other infectious or sexually transmitted diseases;

children.label: VIII
children.text: substance abuse and treatment services; and

children.label: IX
children.text: legal services;

children.label: i
children.text: prevent and respond to violence against women and girls;

children.label: ii
children.text: promote the integration of screening and assessment for gender-based violence into HIV/AIDS programming;

children.label: iii
children.text: promote appropriate HIV/AIDS counseling, testing, and treatment into gender-based violence programs; and

children.label: iv
children.text: assist governments to develop partnerships with civil society organizations to create networks for psychosocial, legal, economic, or other support services;

children.label: i
children.text: address the frequent coinfection of HIV and tuberculosis, in accordance with World Health Organization guidelines;

children.label: ii
children.text: promote provider-initiated or “opt-out” HIV/AIDS counseling and testing and appropriate referral for treatment and care to individuals with tuberculosis or its symptoms, particularly in areas with significant HIV prevalence; and

children.label: iii
children.text: strengthen programs to ensure that individuals testing positive for HIV receive tuberculosis screening and to improve laboratory capacities, infection control, and adherence; and

children.label: i
children.text: improve the effectiveness of national responses to HIV/AIDS;

children.label: ii
children.text: strengthen overall health systems in high-prevalence countries, including support for workforce training, retention, and effective deployment, capacity building, laboratory development, equipment maintenance and repair, and public health and related public financial management systems and operations; and

children.label: iii
children.text: encourage fair and transparent procurement practices among partner countries; and

children.label: iv
children.text: promote in-country or intra-regional pediatric training for physicians and other health professionals, preferably through public-private partnerships involving colleges and universities, with the goal of increasing pediatric HIV workforce capacity.

children.label: A
children.text: support the development, implementation, and management of comprehensive HIV/AIDS plans in support of the national HIV/AIDS strategy;

children.label: B
children.text: operate at all times in a manner that emphasizes efficiency, accountability, and results-driven programs;

children.label: C
children.text: engage both local and foreign development partners and donors, including businesses, government agencies, academic institutions, nongovernmental organizations, foundations, multilateral development agencies, and faith-based organizations, to assist the country in coordinating and implementing HIV/AIDS prevention, treatment, and monitoring programs in accordance with its national HIV/AIDS strategy;

children.label: D
children.text: provide technical assistance, consultant services, financial planning, monitoring and evaluation, and research in support of the national HIV/AIDS strategy; and

children.label: E
children.text: establish local human resource capacities for the national HIV/AIDS strategy through the transfer of medical, managerial, leadership, and technical skills.

children.label: A
children.text: cost sharing assurances that meet the requirements under ; andsection 2151h of this title

children.label: B
children.text: transition strategies to ensure sustainability of such programs and activities, including health care systems, under other international donor support, or budget support by respective foreign governments.

children.label: A
children.text: The congressionally mandated Institute of Medicine report entitled “PEPFAR Implementation: Progress and Promise” states: “The next strategy [of the U.S. Global AIDS Initiative] should squarely address the needs and challenges involved in supporting sustainable country HIV/AIDS programs, thereby transitioning from a focus on emergency relief.”.

children.label: B
children.text: One mechanism to promote the transition from an emergency to a public health and development approach to HIV/AIDS is through compacts or framework agreements between the United States Government and each participating nation.

children.label: i
children.text: the United States Government; and

children.label: I
children.text: national or regional entities representing low-income countries served by an existing United States Agency for International Development or Department of Health and Human Services presence or regional platform; or

children.label: aa
children.text: experiencing significantly high HIV prevalence or risk of significantly increasing incidence within the general population;

children.label: bb
children.text: served by an existing United States Agency for International Development or Department of Health and Human Services presence or regional platform; and

children.label: cc
children.text: that have inadequate financial means within such country or region.

children.label: i
children.text: may be made with other countries or regional entities served by an existing United States Agency for International Development or Department of Health and Human Services presence or regional platform;

children.label: ii
children.text: shall require significant investments in HIV prevention, care, and treatment services by the host country;

children.label: iii
children.text: shall be time-limited in terms of United States contributions; and

children.label: I
children.text: justifying the need for such compacts;

children.label: II
children.text: describing the expected investment by the country or regional entity; and

children.label: III
children.text: describing the scope, nature, expected total United States investment, and time frame of the limited technical assistance under the compact and its intended impact.

children.label: i
children.text: promote local and national efforts to reduce stigma associated with HIV/AIDS; and

children.label: ii
children.text: work with and promote the role of civil society in combating HIV/AIDS.

children.label: D
children.text: Compacts shall take into account the overall national health and development and national HIV/AIDS and public health strategies of each country.

children.label: i
children.text: consideration of the specific objectives that the country and the United States expect to achieve during the term of a compact;

children.label: ii
children.text: consideration of the respective responsibilities of the country and the United States in the achievement of such objectives;

children.label: iii
children.text: consideration of regular benchmarks to measure progress toward achieving such objectives;

children.label: iv
children.text: an identification of the intended beneficiaries, disaggregated by gender and age, and including information on orphans and vulnerable children, to the maximum extent practicable;

children.label: I
children.text: address the factors that put women and girls at greater risk of HIV/AIDS; and

children.label: II
children.text: strengthen elements such as the economic, educational, and social status of women, girls, orphans, and vulnerable children and the inheritance rights and safety of such individuals;

children.label: I
children.text: strengthen the health care capacity, including factors such as the training, retention, deployment, recruitment, and utilization of health care workers;

children.label: II
children.text: improve supply chain management; and

children.label: III
children.text: improve the health systems and infrastructure of the partner country, including the ability of compact participants to maintain and operate equipment transferred or purchased as part of the compact;

children.label: vii
children.text: consideration of proposed mechanisms to provide oversight;

children.label: viii
children.text: consideration of the role of civil society in the development of a compact and the achievement of its objectives;

children.label: ix
children.text: a description of the current and potential participation of other donors in the achievement of such objectives, as appropriate; and

children.label: x
children.text: consideration of a plan to ensure appropriate fiscal accountability for the use of assistance.

children.label: F
children.text: For regional compacts, priority shall be given to countries that are included in regional funds and programs in existence as of .July 30, 2008

children.label: i
children.text: cost sharing assurances that meet the requirements under ; andsection 2151h of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: transition strategies to ensure sustainability of such programs and activities, including health care systems, under other international donor support, and budget support by respective foreign governments.

children.label: A
children.text: takes into account the local perspectives of the rural and urban poor, including women, in each country; and

children.label: B
children.text: consults with private and voluntary organizations, including faith-based organizations, the business community, and other donors in the country.

children.heading: Congressional and public notification after entering into a compact
children.label: 4 Congressional and public notification after entering into a compact
i
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: publish such information in the Federal Register and on the Internet website of the Office of the Global AIDS Coordinator.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than , and annually thereafter, the President shall submit to the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate and the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives a report in an open, machine readable format, on the implementation of this section for the prior fiscal year.February 15, 2014

children.heading: Report due in 2014
children.label: 2 Report due in 2014
children.text: The report due not later than , shall include the elements required by law prior to the enactment of the PEPFAR Stewardship and Oversight Act of 2013.February 15, 2014

children.label: i
children.text: estimates by partner country of the global burden and need; and

children.label: ii
children.text: HIV incidence, prevalence, and AIDS deaths for the reporting period.

children.label: I
children.text: ensure that the annual increase in new patients on antiretroviral treatment exceeds the number of annual new HIV infections;

children.label: II
children.text: reduce the number of new HIV infections below the number of deaths among persons infected with HIV; and

children.label: III
children.text: achieve an AIDS-free generation;

children.label: I

children.label: D
children.text: A description of how United States-funded programs, in conjunction with the Global Fund, other donors, and partner countries, together set targets, measure progress, and achieve positive outcomes in partner countries.

children.label: E
children.text: An annual assessment of outcome indicator development, dissemination, and performance for programs supported under this section, including ongoing corrective actions to improve reporting.

children.label: F

children.label: R
children.text: A description of program evaluations completed during the reporting period, including whether all completed evaluations have been published on a publically available Internet website and whether any completed evaluations did not adhere to the common evaluation standards of practice published under paragraph (4).

children.heading: Common evaluation standards
children.label: 4 Common evaluation standards
children.text: Not later than , the Global AIDS Coordinator shall publish on a publically available Internet website the common evaluation standards of practice referred to in paragraph (3)(R).February 1, 2014

children.heading: Partner country defined
children.label: 5 Partner country defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “partner country” means a country with a minimum United States Government investment of HIV/AIDS assistance of at least $5,000,000 in the prior fiscal year.

children.heading: Funding limitation
children.label: g Funding limitation
children.text: Of the funds made available to carry out this section in any fiscal year, not more than 7 percent may be used for the administrative expenses of the United States Agency for International Development in support of activities described in , this section, , and . Such amount shall be in addition to other amounts otherwise available for such purposes.section 2151b(c) of this titlesection 2151b–3 of this titlesection 2151b–4 of this title

children.heading: AIDS
children.label: 1 AIDS
children.text: The term “AIDS” means acquired immune deficiency syndrome.

children.heading: HIV
children.label: 2 HIV
children.text: The term “HIV” means the human immunodeficiency virus, the pathogen that causes AIDS.

children.heading: HIV/AIDS
children.label: 3 HIV/AIDS
children.text: The term “HIV/AIDS” means, with respect to an individual, an individual who is infected with HIV or living with AIDS.

children.heading: Relevant executive branch agencies
children.label: 4 Relevant executive branch agencies
children.text: The term “relevant executive branch agencies” means the Department of State, the United States Agency for International Development, the Department of Health and Human Services (including its agencies and offices), and any other department or agency of the United States that participates in international HIV/AIDS activities pursuant to the authorities of such department or agency or this chapter.
